roadmap.title: Start every long counter
roadmap.description: Advance the opening story, repair Bancho Sushi, complete the gun and iDiver tutorials, and begin collecting materials, catching fish with each weapon class, feeding Momo, researching dishes, enhancing one sustainable sushi, and raising Cooksta rank.

roadmap.title: Clear the story and protect the missable ration
roadmap.description: Follow the main missions through the Sea People Village, Glacial Passage, Glacial Area, and ending. During the Chapter 6 Sea Blue base infiltration, eat the ration can on the final stealth floor before leaving; this is the only action that cannot be recovered in postgame.

roadmap.title: Complete village, farm, photo, and boss side content
roadmap.description: Finish Mima, Gumo, Duwa, Momo, Otto, Udo, Sato, and storm-night requests. Photograph all eight murals, defeat the twelve required creature bosses, raise five adult GYAO!, catch five FishMon, collect weapon blueprints, and reroll Glacial Area dives until Mjolnir appears.

roadmap.title: Finish restaurant and cumulative cleanup
roadmap.description: Use postgame profits and routine dives to finish 20 cat feedings, 200 workshop sales, both maximum farm expansions, Platinum and Diamond Cooksta ranks, 30 researched dishes, one level-20 employee, and the branch restaurant.

achievements.summary: Earn 100 gold during the opening and pay the restaurant repair balance to restore @loc{Bancho Sushi}.

guide.context: The repair is part of the opening story and cannot be skipped; the negative balance disappears when the required earnings have covered it.

steps.text: Catch fish during the tutorial dives, place their sushi on Bancho's menu, complete restaurant service, and continue until the 100-gold repair cost is paid.

achievements.summary: Complete @npc{Cobra} and @npc{Duff}'s first underwater-gun tutorial near the beginning of the story.

guide.context: The tutorial introduces the weapon system before free weapon crafting and upgrading become available.

steps.text: Follow the opening mission prompts, equip the supplied gun, aim at the marked underwater target, and complete every firing prompt.

achievements.summary: Follow the dolphins and complete all 3 daytime rescue encounters in the @loc{Blue Hole}.

guide.context: The encounters appear across separate morning or afternoon dives, and their event order can vary. The third completed rescue also awards a charm.

steps.text: Whenever a dolphin appears near the start of a daytime dive, follow it and finish the requested rescue sequence.
Repeat on later daytime dives until the third dolphin encounter is completed and the charm is awarded.

achievements.summary: Buy any first equipment upgrade from the @mechanic{iDiver} phone app.

guide.context: The opening story supplies the iDiver tutorial, so no optional quest is needed before the first purchase.

steps.text: Open the smartphone, select iDiver, choose an affordable suit, oxygen tank, cargo box, or harpoon upgrade, and confirm the purchase.

achievements.summary: Rescue @npc{Ramo} and @npc{Suwam}, then escort them into the @loc{Sea People Village} during the main story.

guide.context: The village visit follows the first exploration of the Blue Hole Depths and is mandatory story progression.

steps.text: Complete the Depths story objectives involving Ramo and Suwam, protect them during the return route, and enter the Sea People Village with them.

achievements.description: Entered the Deep Sea for the first time.
achievements.summary: Descend into the @loc{Blue Hole Depths} for the first time during the early main story.

guide.context: A sufficiently upgraded diving suit is needed for deeper water, and the story points Dave toward the route when it becomes relevant.

steps.text: Advance the main missions, upgrade the diving suit when prompted, and swim through the deep-water route until the Blue Hole Depths location appears.

achievements.description: Researched 5 new dishes at Bancho Sushi.
achievements.summary: Spend @item{Artisan's Flames} in Bancho Sushi's Research menu to unlock 5 new dishes.

guide.context: Successful nightly service awards Artisan's Flames; choosing low-cost recipes preserves flames for the later 30-dish milestone.

steps.text: Earn Artisan's Flames from restaurant service, open the Research menu before service, and confirm five recipe researches.

achievements.summary: Use Bancho Sushi's Enhance menu to improve dishes a total of 5 times.

guide.context: Repeated enhancements require increasing ingredient quantities. A common single-fish sushi is easier to sustain than a multi-ingredient recipe.

steps.text: Stock a common fish in repeated dives, open the restaurant's Ingredients or Menu screen, select its sushi, and choose Enhance whenever the required quantity is available.
Complete five enhancement actions; they may be applied to one dish or split among several dishes.

achievements.summary: Open Bancho Sushi's @mechanic{Interior} menu and buy any first decoration.

guide.context: Decoration price and placement do not affect credit, so the least expensive unlocked item is sufficient.

steps.text: Enter the restaurant before service, open Interior, select an affordable decoration, and confirm the purchase.

achievements.summary: Reach 10 Cooksta followers and claim @mechanic{Bronze rank} in the Cooksta app.

guide.context: Followers are earned through successful restaurant nights; the promotion must be completed from the Cooksta phone app after the target is met.

steps.text: Serve customers without running out of prepared dishes until Cooksta shows at least 10 followers.
Open Cooksta on the smartphone and confirm the Bronze-rank promotion.

achievements.description: Picked up 100 items at the Blue Hole.
achievements.summary: Pick up 100 crafting materials, such as scrap, rope, glass, and screws, across @loc{Blue Hole} dives.

guide.context: The counter is cumulative across dives, but opening supply or weapon boxes does not count as an item pickup.

steps.text: During every dive, collect loose crafting objects lying on the seabed until 100 qualifying material pickups have registered.

achievements.summary: Damage and capture any shark for the first time in the @loc{Blue Hole}.

guide.context: An early VIP mission places a Whitetip Reefshark at the far-right side of the shallows, creating a reliable first encounter.

steps.text: Carry a loaded gun into the early shark mission, stay outside the shark's charge line, and damage it between attacks until the capture completes.

achievements.description: Caught 10 fish with a Sniper Rifle.
achievements.summary: Capture 10 fish using finishing damage from any @item{Sniper Rifle} variant.

guide.context: The basic and Lightning Sniper Rifles qualify; small shallow fish reduce ammunition and damage requirements.

steps.text: Equip a Sniper Rifle before diving, shoot small fish in the shallows, and ensure the rifle delivers each finishing hit until ten captures register.

achievements.summary: Capture 20 fish using finishing hits from melee weapons.

guide.context: A katana, baseball bat, or other strong temporary melee weapon from an underwater box finishes fish faster than the starting knife.

steps.text: Equip a melee weapon, approach small shallow fish, and strike until the melee weapon receives capture credit for 20 fish.

achievements.description: First time bringing Dave back from the brink of death.
achievements.summary: Run out of oxygen underwater once and accept @npc{Cobra}'s rescue.

guide.context: A rescue ends the dive and allows only one carried item to be retained, so attempt this with an expendable inventory.

steps.text: Begin a low-value dive, remain underwater until Dave's oxygen reaches zero, and choose the rescue option when Cobra arrives.

achievements.summary: Raise Sea People trust, open the rear village route, and enter the @loc{Glacial Passage}.

guide.context: The entrance becomes available after village reputation missions and is reached near the mirror portal at the back of the Sea People Village.

steps.text: Complete the available Sea People requests until the story opens the rear passage, then travel through the village portal and enter the Glacial Passage.

achievements.description: Got to know Momo a little better.
achievements.summary: Feed @npc{Momo} until his nighttime quest appears, then follow him to discover his secret.

guide.context: Cat food is commonly found in red cooking pots during dives. Momo can be fed before service but is absent once the restaurant opens at night.

steps.text: Bring cat food to Bancho Sushi and feed Momo before nighttime service whenever the interaction is available.
When Momo's nighttime event begins, follow him without abandoning the sequence and complete the discovery scene.

achievements.description: Entered the Glacial Area for the first time.
achievements.summary: Solve the @loc{Glacial Passage} route and escape the @enemy{Giant Gadon} chase into the @loc{Glacial Area}.

guide.context: This is mandatory late-story progression after entering Glacial Passage; suit upgrades are needed for the area's depth and cold route.

steps.text: Complete each mirror, light, and passage puzzle in the Glacial Passage, then keep moving through the forced Giant Gadon chase until Dave reaches the Glacial Area.

achievements.summary: Finish the main story and watch the ending credits through their conclusion.

guide.context: The completed save continues into postgame, so unfinished side content and cumulative achievements remain available afterward.

steps.text: Complete the Ancient Control Room finale, defeat the final story encounter, and remain through the ending credits until normal play resumes.

achievements.summary: Complete @npc{Mima}'s Sea People Village request chain until her restaurant opens.

guide.context: Mima's later errands appear after the initial reputation work and include obtaining a seaweed supply from the Depths.

steps.text: Speak with Mima whenever her village quest marker appears, complete each requested delivery, and return to her after the final seaweed-supply task.

achievements.summary: Complete @npc{Gumo}'s village requests and advance the story until his @loc{seaweed farm} opens.

guide.context: Gumo is located above Duwa's workshop and is marked on the Sea People Village map; his farm is unlocked after both reputation and story requirements are satisfied.

steps.text: Accept and finish every quest marker at Gumo, then continue the main story and revisit him when the seaweed-plot event becomes available.

achievements.summary: Complete @npc{Duwa}'s village requests, including the later soup delivery, to open his workshop.

guide.context: The soup request appears later than some other village errands, so watch for a new exclamation-mark quest at Duwa's location.

steps.text: Finish Duwa's available requests, accept his later illness quest, obtain Bancho's soup as directed, and deliver it to Duwa.

achievements.summary: Collect every base weapon blueprint shown in @npc{Duff}'s Weapon Shop.

guide.context: Weapon boxes yield blueprint parts, and multiple finds can be required before a weapon's blueprint completes. Returning to the boat generates new box contents on the next dive.

steps.text: Check Duff's Weapon Shop for incomplete base weapons, then open every weapon box encountered during dives to collect their blueprint parts.

For the late Ice Gun blueprint, farm weapon boxes in the cold @loc{Glacial Area}; for the late Sticky Bomb Gun blueprint, farm boxes in the hot @loc{Hydrothermal Vents}. Return to the boat between searches until both complete.

achievements.summary: Confirm 3 gun enhancements in @npc{Duff}'s Weapon Shop.

guide.context: The three enhancements do not need to belong to one weapon, so inexpensive upgrades across different guns can reduce material farming.

steps.text: Collect the displayed weapon materials and gold, open Duff's Weapon Shop, select Enhance, and confirm three available gun upgrades.

achievements.description: Captured all of the Sea People murals.
achievements.summary: Photograph all 8 @loc{Sea People murals} and return to the boat for @npc{Dr. Bacon}'s completion scene.

guide.context: Use the Album phone app to check progress. Seven murals are in Glacial Passage, and several become photographable only after story puzzles alter the route.

steps.text: Photograph the mandatory mural in the Sea People Record Chamber during Beyond the Rock Pile.
In Glacial Passage, photograph the first-floor entrance mural and the mural beyond the first shifting-wall puzzle room.
On the second floor, photograph the mural near the mirror and frozen opening, then backtrack into the laser puzzle room after Dave is moved out to photograph its mural.
On the third floor, photograph the mural after repairing the angled mirror and the mural beyond the next laser-mirror puzzle.
After escaping Giant Gadon, revisit its former prison room, photograph the final mural, and return to the boat to trigger Dr. Bacon's scene.

achievements.description: Caught 300 fish at the Blue Hole.
achievements.summary: Capture a cumulative total of 300 fish across @loc{Blue Hole} dives.

guide.context: The total persists across the save. Small schools are efficient because they avoid long capture QTEs, while the underwater drone frees weight by sending large catches to the surface.

steps.text: Catch every convenient small fish and school during routine ingredient dives, use the drone when heavy catches fill the cargo limit, and continue until the 300th capture registers.

achievements.description: Eat the ration can during the Sea Blue base infiltration.
achievements.summary: During the Chapter 6 @loc{Sea Blue base} infiltration, eat the ration can on the final stealth floor before leaving.

guide.context: @warn{This is the only missable base-game achievement.} The base cannot be freely revisited after the story infiltration, so a missed can requires another save.

steps.text: During the Infiltrate the Sea Blue Base mission, progress through the stealth floors by hiding in refrigerators and behind boxes.
On the final floor, locate the ration can lying on the ground, interact with it to eat it, and verify the achievement before finishing the infiltration.

achievements.summary: Use cat food to feed the cats at @loc{Bancho Sushi} on 20 separate daily opportunities.

guide.context: The cats are present before nighttime service and accept food only once per in-game day. Red cooking pots during dives are a recurring source of cat food.

steps.text: Open red cooking pots during dives and keep every cat-food can found.
Before restaurant service, interact with the cats at Bancho Sushi and feed them once that day; repeat until 20 feedings have registered.

achievements.summary: Sell 200 individual materials through @npc{Duwa}'s workshop in the Sea People Village.

guide.context: The counter uses item quantity rather than transaction count. Common scrap and screws are suitable, but reserve rare materials needed for weapon enhancements.

steps.text: Collect excess common materials during dives, open Duwa's workshop, choose Sell, and sell stacks whose combined item count reaches 200.

achievements.summary: After @npc{Sammy} returns to the farm, buy and install a sprinkler from @npc{Otto}'s shop.

guide.context: The sprinkler purchase appears only after the farm storyline reunites Sammy with Otto; buying ordinary seeds or watering by hand does not qualify.

steps.text: Complete Otto and Sammy's farm requests until Sammy resumes work, open Otto's farm shop, select a sprinkler upgrade, and confirm installation.

achievements.summary: Raise 5 virtual fish to their adult form in the @mechanic{GYAO!} phone minigame.

guide.context: GYAO! unlocks the day after the second Stormy Night sequence. The five adults may be identical, and each adult can be rebirthed immediately before starting the next cycle.

steps.text: After Sato unlocks GYAO!, check the phone pet between time periods and maintain its food, affection, health, cleanliness, and training until it reaches the final adult form.
Use the rebirth option after adulthood, raise the replacement pet through the same full cycle, and repeat until five adults have been counted.

achievements.description: Took 10 photos at Photo Spots in the Blue Hole.
achievements.summary: Complete 10 camera-marked photo spots during @npc{Udo}'s Blue Hole assignments.

guide.context: New photo assignments appear as story progress and days advance. Sea People murals use a separate Album collection and do not replace these Blue Hole photo spots.

steps.text: Accept Udo's photo requests, dive into the indicated region, swim to the camera icon, and complete the framing or timing prompt at each spot.
Finish ten separate Blue Hole photo spots across the available assignments.

achievements.summary: Capture the first 5 weekly @enemy{FishMon} targets during their Saturday-morning-to-Sunday-evening assignments.

guide.context: Complete enough of Udo's ocean-life photo requests for him to install the FishMon app. He then assigns one target per weekend, so the achievement takes five in-game weeks.

steps.text: For the Warrior Parrotfish, approach from behind and attack its vulnerable rear until it can be recovered.
For the Wizard Barrel Jellyfish, stay outside its reaction range and use a long-range tranquilizer, such as a tranquilizing Sniper Rifle, before recovering it alive.
For the Poison Ghost Squid, shine an underwater UV light on it to expose the body, then tranquilize and recover it.
For the Silver Titan Triggerfish, use a weapon with lightning damage to disable its defense, then recover the target.
For the Spotted Moray Eel, begin a night dive during its assigned weekend, locate it with the Fish Tracker, stun it with ordinary weapon fire, and recover it before Sunday evening.

achievements.description: Destroy every developer-name target in the post-credits space minigame.
achievements.summary: Shoot every developer-name target before time expires in the post-credits @mechanic{space minigame}.

guide.context: If the credits attempt fails, the minigame becomes replayable from Dave's phone on the following day. The arena wraps at its edges, and the scoreboard displays how many names remain.

steps.text: During the ending minigame or its phone replay, ignore ordinary enemies and continuously shoot the floating developer-name targets.
Move downward and slightly right to loop through the wrapping arena, watch the remaining-name counter, and destroy every name before the timer ends.

achievements.description: Defeat all 12 creature bosses required by the base game.
achievements.summary: Defeat the 12 required story and side-quest creature bosses, including all storm-night whirlpool bosses.

guide.context: The base achievement counts creature bosses, not every later-content encounter. The optional Lusca is not required; use the boss cards in Marinca to identify missing fights.

steps.text: Complete the main story bosses: Giant Squid, Giant Wolf Eel, Goblin Shark, Giant Gadon, Phantom Jellyfish, Helicoprion, Kronosaurus, and Yawie.
Complete Maki's Catch Clione request and defeat the Clione Queen triggered by the fifth clione.
On stormy nights, enter the marked whirlpools and defeat Truck Hermit Crab, Mantis Shrimp, and Great White Shark Klaus through their associated side quests.
Check Marinca for all 12 boss cards after the final fight and complete any missing base-game encounter.

achievements.summary: Buy every size expansion for @loc{Otto's rice field} until it reaches maximum area.

guide.context: The final expansion is expensive, so postgame restaurant profits can fund it without delaying more useful early upgrades.

steps.text: Advance Otto's farm storyline, open his upgrade shop whenever a new rice-field expansion appears, and purchase every size tier through the maximum.

achievements.summary: Buy every size expansion for @loc{Otto's vegetable field} until it reaches maximum area.

guide.context: This is separate from the rice-field expansion and sprinkler purchase; all vegetable-plot size tiers must be bought.

steps.text: Open Otto's farm upgrade shop and purchase each vegetable-field expansion in order until no larger field tier remains.

achievements.description: Capture a fish using Mjolnir in the Glacial Area.
achievements.summary: Find @item{Mjolnir} frozen in the @loc{Glacial Area}, break it free, and use its melee hit to capture a fish.

guide.context: Mjolnir is a random rare spawn in an ore-like ice block. It can appear in the main Glacial Area, Caves I or III, or the control-room route; returning to the boat rerolls the dive layout.

steps.text: Enter the Glacial Area and inspect ore spawn points near the Greenland Shark and Porbeagle routes, inside Caves I and III, and toward the control room for a hammer encased in ice.
If Mjolnir is absent, return to the boat and begin another Glacial Area dive to generate new spawns.
Break the ice block, equip Mjolnir as the current melee weapon, find a weak fish, and deliver the finishing hit with the hammer.

achievements.summary: Meet 200 followers, 250 Best Taste, and 19 researched dishes, then claim @mechanic{Platinum Cooksta rank}.

guide.context: Cooksta promotion checks all three displayed requirements. Repeatedly enhancing one sustainable single-fish sushi is an efficient way to raise Best Taste.

steps.text: Run successful restaurant nights until Cooksta shows at least 200 followers, research at least 19 dishes, and enhance one dish until its Best Taste reaches 250.
Open the Cooksta app and confirm the Platinum-rank promotion.

achievements.summary: Spend training fees to raise any single Bancho Sushi employee to level 20.

guide.context: The final training levels cost close to 100,000 gold in total progression, so concentrating profits on one useful employee avoids duplicating the expense.

steps.text: Choose one regularly used employee, open the Staff menu after profitable services, select Training, and repeatedly pay for that employee's next level until level 20 is reached.

achievements.summary: Meet 720 followers, 375 Best Taste, and 32 researched dishes, then claim @mechanic{Diamond Cooksta rank}.

guide.context: Diamond is the final Cooksta promotion and checks all three requirements together. Farmed fish from the Fish Farm can sustain repeated enhancements of one high-value sushi.

steps.text: Continue successful restaurant nights to 720 followers, research at least 32 dishes, and enhance one reliable sushi until its Best Taste reaches 375.
Open Cooksta and confirm the Diamond-rank promotion after all three requirements are marked complete.

achievements.description: Researched 30 new dishes at Bancho Sushi.
achievements.summary: Spend @item{Artisan's Flames} to research 30 new dishes at Bancho Sushi.

guide.context: Low-flame recipes advance the counter most efficiently. More choices unlock from employee training, VIP stories, bosses, new fish ingredients, and Jango.

steps.text: Earn Artisan's Flames from well-rated restaurant nights, unlock additional recipe choices through side content and staff training, and purchase the cheapest available researches until 30 dishes have been unlocked.

achievements.description: Bancho Sushi formed a branch restaurant.
achievements.summary: Finish the late VIP storyline and establish Bancho Sushi's @loc{branch restaurant}.

guide.context: The branch feature appears after the VIP customer sequence resolves Bancho's old dispute; it is a restaurant-progression unlock rather than a free early purchase.

steps.text: Complete every VIP customer event offered at Bancho Sushi, continue the resulting Bancho story through its resolution, and follow the branch-management tutorial to open the new restaurant.
